facts.text: Seabrook–Wilson House, nicknamed the “Spy House,” was built in 1663 and is the oldest structure in Monmouth County, located in Port Monmouth.
All Saints Memorial Episcopal Church, erected in 1864 in Navesink, earned National Historic Landmark status for its architectural and historical significance.
Belford emerged in 1891 when a railroad station and post office opened east of Port Monmouth, giving the area its name.
The Monmouth County Historical Association, founded in 1898 by educator Caroline Gallup Reed, preserves and shares the county’s rich heritage.
Middletown Township, where Port Monmouth sits, was officially founded in 1664 as a colonial seat, with settlers arriving from western Long Island and New England.
